statement.p: One rectangular wall in your classroom is {14.5\ {\rm ft}} longer than it is tall. The total area of the wall is {228\ {\rm ft^{2}}}\text{.} How tall is the wall?

solution.p: The area of a rectangle is given by multipying its two dimensions. If we let h stand for the height of the wall, then {h+14.5} is the width, and the total area is {h\mathopen{}\left(h+14.5\right)}\text{.} So we must solve the equation {h\mathopen{}\left(h+14.5\right)}=228\text{.}
After multiplying and moving to standard form, we have {h^{2}+14.5h-228}=0\text{.} Using the quadratic formula:
Only the solution h\approx{9.5} makes sense in this situation. The wall is {9.5\ {\rm ft}} tall.
